title: examine
text: examine Display the contents of a field, function, or variable, or change the contents of variables or selected ProcScript functions. Object Sytax for Examine Syntax for Change Fields: Examine Field { .Entity } Not applicable ProcScript functions: Examine Function { ( Name ) } Function { ( Name ) } Examine ProcFunction = Value ProcFunction = Value Component, global, and general variables: Examine Variable Variable Examine Variable = Value Variable = Value General variables ($1-$99) only: Examine Number Examine Examine Number = Value Local variables: Examine Variable Examine Number = Value Arguments Field .Entity —display the field value; if .Entity is omitted, the current field is assumed. Function —display the current value of the specified ProcScript function. Some functions cannot be examined. Variable —variable name or number. ProcFunction —one of $status , $result , $selblk , or $format . Value —set the function or variable to this value. Description The examine command enables you to see the current value of a field, function, or variable. When used with =Value it can be used to set the value variables and selected ProcScript functions. Except for fields and local variables, the examine keyword can often be omitted. Examining and Setting Variables Specifying a Variable without = Value displays the current value of the variable. When examine is used in the command, general variables can be referenced by number, without the leading dollar sign ($). When no arguments are specified, examine displays the contents of the next sequentially numbered general variable. Within the debugger, the ProcScript functions $status , $result , $selblk , and $format can also be referred to as $100 , $101 , $102 , and $103 respectively. Excluded Functions Functions that cannot be examined in the character mode debugger include: $logical $text —the value of a specific message can be examined using the debugger message command. $textexist $web $subsetreturn $fielddbvalue $clock $condition $expression $date $datim $number $syntax $time Example: Examining Data Display the value of the field NAME.HORSE: e name.horse Copy Display the name of the current entity: ex $entname Copy Display the field modification status of the field NAME.HORSE: $fieldmod(name.horse) Copy Display the value of the component variable $TEMP$: $temp$ Copy Display the value of the local variable myvar1 : e myvar1 Copy Display the value of general variables $79 and $81: e 79 $81 Copy Example: Changing Values of Variables and ProcScript Functions Set the value of the component variable $TEMP$: $temp$=0 Copy Set the value of global variable $$CITY: $$city=Amsterdam Copy Set the value of general variable $47: ex 47 = 14.2 Copy Set the value of local variable TOTAL: e total = 0 Copy Set the value of $status : 4100 = -1 Copy Related Links message Display the text of a message.
